metrics.narrative: Pain and value drivers are qualitatively well articulated across the three 2025 demo sessions: manual CLM redlining and metadata effort (touchpoint 2, 25 Jun 2025), manual supplier onboarding via Ariba with credit/adverse-media checks (touchpoint 2), and invoices with no PO/contract linkage causing compliance gaps on the $20,000 threshold (touchpoint 3, 26 Jun 2025, buyer speaker: "Contract compliance is required for purchases over $20,000, but many invoices still do not tie back to purchase orders or contracts"). No buyer-quantified dollar savings or ROI figure has been captured in any read source; the closest is a hypothetical illustrative example from a Zycus rep ($1M vs $5M contract budgeting), not a buyer commitment.

champion.narrative: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai) is the deal's operating champion: he arranged the Jan 2026 onsite, offered/coordinated the ANA POC discussion, connected the team to leadership on next steps (26 May 2026), and has been the sole conduit to Allstate leadership throughout. He is currently at risk: he has not responded to three consecutive Zycus follow-ups (8 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026) on the single most important open question, the SAP Ariba beta outcome, since a genuine two-way email exchange on 22 May 2026 (48 days ago as of this sweep). What developed: consistent scheduling support and internal advocacy through May 2026. What is missing: any response for over six weeks, and no evidence he has ever brokered direct access to Casey McDowell despite being asked to since February 2026.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is confirmed as the incumbent and dominant competitive threat: Karson's 15 Dec 2025 note reads "confirmed SAP VS Zycus" as the explicit head-to-head framing, and Allstate has been running its own internal SAP Ariba workshop/beta program since at least 26 May 2026 as the direct alternative to switching, with the decision gated on that beta's outcome. Coupa and Ivalua appear only in the Salesforce Competitors field with no corroborating mention in any of the five read Avoma calls or the Next Step trail; treat them as background/dormant. Fieldglass (Allstate's contingent-labor incumbent, touchpoint 4) and Beeline (named as an alternative VMS) are adjacent-category considerations, not S2P suite competitors. iCertis surfaced as a single passing CLM question in touchpoint 2 (25 Jun 2025) with no follow-through.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is directly and repeatedly confirmed by named Allstate speakers across all three 2025 demo sessions: manual Word-based contract redlining and check-in/out (touchpoint 2, 25 Jun 2025), manual supplier onboarding and risk validation via Ariba (touchpoint 2), invoices with no PO/contract tie-back especially for tax payments (touchpoint 3, 26 Jun 2025), and a fragmented tech landscape spanning Ariba, SAP Vendor Invoice Management, and a manual SharePoint timesheet process for contingent labor (touchpoint 4, 15 Jul 2025: "The prospect seeks to consolidate its technology landscape by integrating disparate systems into a unified source-to-pay solution").

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or contracting motion has started; the deal has not moved commercially since Vendor Selected-adjacent activity. Even the commercial proposal refresh flagged as needed on 28 Apr 2026 ("FU on all pending items - POC, Reference, and updated proposal") shows no evidence of having been sent as of the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step entry.

economic_buyer.narrative: Casey McDowell (VP, Global Enterprise Procurement) is tagged Executive Sponsor and Cheryl Harris (SVP, Procurement) is tagged Executive Sponsor/Evaluator on the Opportunity Contact Roles, so both are formally identified. Neither has appeared on any of the five Avoma touchpoints read for this deal. Karson has been trying to secure a Casey session since at least 4 Feb 2026 ("working on setting up executive session with Casey in Chicago") and again 18 Feb 2026 ("asking for executive meeting w Casey/cheryl"), with no evidence in the Next Step trail that either meeting ever occurred. Engagement with power remains entirely indirect, routed through Gaurav.

decision_process.narrative: On 21 Jan 2026 the buyer stated a decision was expected "in next 3/4 months" (i.e. by roughly Apr-May 2026), tied to the expiry of Allstate's Ariba renewal 12 months out. That self-set window has passed with no new date communicated. Since then the process has become entirely gated on Allstate's own internal SAP Ariba beta/workshop program, first noted as still running on 26 May 2026, with Gaurav providing no outcome as of 16 Jun or 29 Jun 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal criteria document is referenced in any read source. Implied criteria surface in touchpoint 1 (23 Jun 2025) where the Zycus rep probes Allstate's RFP scoring process and business involvement in evaluation, and throughout the deal the recurring emphasis is on AI capability parity/superiority versus SAP (the whole 21 Jan 2026 onsite centered on this), integration ease with SAP S/4HANA and Workday, and reference-proof of AI usage at scale.

win_position.text: The buyer is actively running its own SAP Ariba renewal beta/workshop as the direct alternative to switching, and has not shared the outcome despite Zycus asking on 8 Jun, 16 Jun, and 29 Jun 2026.
Pain is confirmed and well-documented across three 2025 demo sessions, and Allstate voluntarily hosted a 1.5-hour in-person onsite in Chicago/India on 21 Jan 2026 to compare Zycus vs SAP directly.
Neither mapped Executive Sponsor (Casey McDowell, Cheryl Harris) has ever appeared on a call; access to power remains entirely indirect through the champion.

deal_momentum.text: The last genuine two-way buyer touch was an inbound email on 22 May 2026 (48 days ago); since then three Zycus follow-ups (8 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026) have gone unanswered.
The 5-reference-call commitment and updated commercial proposal, both flagged as pending since late April 2026, remain undelivered.

customer_commitment.text: Allstate's champion (Gaurav) previously drove real forward motion: arranging the January onsite, requesting the ANA POC, and connecting Zycus to leadership on 26 May 2026.
Allstate itself set a 3-4 month decision window on 21 Jan 2026 that has now passed with no new date given.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus has invested heavily in an AI-forward narrative for this deal: the ANA (autonomous negotiation) POC offer, an agent-based intake manager, a McAfee-based innovation proof point shared 29 Jun 2026, and a deliberate reference-strategy pivot to AI-at-scale customers. The gap is proof, not positioning: the POC has not started and the AI-focused references have not been scheduled.

items.quote: Significant AI advancements have been made in the Source-to-Pay suite, including a new agent-based intake manager and an autonomous negotiation flow to drive tactical savings.
The demo showcased robust AI-powered CLM capabilities including automated redlining assistance, a clause/template library, and seamless Microsoft Word integration.
Zycus does not offer a full contingent labor (VMS) management solution like Fieldglass, but can integrate with such systems.
Zycus will replace three unsuitable references and schedule five new calls, two of which showcase customers actively using AI at scale.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Allstate's stated needs center on consolidating a fragmented tech stack (Ariba, SAP VIM, SharePoint, Fieldglass) into one S2P platform with strong AI capability; Zycus has been positioned favorably on AI/roadmap but has not closed out several proof-point commitments.

hard.ais_why:
• The 1/21/2026 Allstate on-site captures a direct buyer-driven AI action: Zycus to schedule and prep two reference calls with customers actively using Zycus AI capabilities, and share the reference list and call details with Allstate. The buyer is asking specifically for AI references, not generic ones.
• Same call records significant AI advancements across the S2P suite (a new agent-based intake manager and an autonomous negotiation flow for tactical savings) and notes that Zycus scrapped three non-AI references and lined up two new customers with high AI adoption appetite. The buyer pushed for that swap.
• An active ANA POC track is documented across 2/25, 3/2, and 4/13/2026, with a planned discussion of the next reference, the ANA POC, and updated commercials. The $1.5M deal is replacing SAP Ariba, with executive sessions in India and Chicago, Merlin in the product set, and an 11/24/2025 entry waiting on a response about key AI data and next steps.
